case_summary: User says 'gate motor not working' but the described mechanism is a ceiling-mounted overhead opener — actually a garage door.

ground_truth.notes: Disambiguation: the user mislabels the equipment ('gate motor') but the mechanical description (ceiling-mounted opener, rail along the ceiling, chain drive) is unmistakeably a garage door. Tests that the system reasons from the described mechanism rather than blindly trusting the user-supplied label. Acceptable alternative: requires_clarification_expected=true.

inputs.user_text: Our gate motor isn't working. It's mounted on the ceiling of our garage and there's a long rail running along the ceiling with a chain. When we press the remote the motor unit on the ceiling makes a clicking sound but nothing moves. The door panels themselves look fine.
